tooltip: 10 principles for building canonical Silvery apps — use high-level components, semantic tokens, and canonical patterns
Primary layout component — CSS flexbox container with borders, padding, scrolling
Styled text component with color, bold, italic, underline, and wrapping
Interactive keyboard-navigable list — j/k, arrows, Enter, type-ahead search
Single-line text input with built-in readline (Ctrl+A/E/K/U, Alt+B/F)
Multi-line text editing with cursor movement, selection, and word wrap
Virtualized list with pluggable cache, navigation, and search
Efficient rendering for large lists — only visible items are mounted
VS Code-style fuzzy command palette with keyboard navigation
Determinate/indeterminate progress indicator with theming
Animated loading indicator with customizable frames
Column-aligned table component with headers and auto-sizing
Tabbed navigation component with keyboard support
Modal overlay with focus trapping and automatic theme
Streaming content display for chat, logs, and agent output
Streaming list where completed items freeze into terminal scrollback
Expandable/collapsible tree navigation component
Resizable split panes component
Auto-dismiss notification component
Styled status label with variant colors (success, error, warning, primary)
Horizontal line separator with optional title label
Clickable OSC 8 hyperlink component — opens URLs in the user's browser
Focusable button with press handler, color variants, and active state
Boolean switch with label — keyboard-togglable on/off control
Vertical form layout with labeled fields, validation, and submit handling
Catches render errors in children and displays a fallback UI
Displays captured console.log/warn/error output inside the TUI
Placeholder loading indicator — animated shimmer effect for content not yet loaded
Modal selection dialog — fuzzy-searchable list with keyboard navigation
Navigation breadcrumb trail with configurable separator
Integrated search input — activates via / or Ctrl+F with match highlighting
React context provider that sets the active color theme for all descendants
Root layout component — fills the terminal viewport with flexbox column layout
Inline terminal image via Kitty graphics protocol or Sixel encoding
Measures available space and passes dimensions to children via render prop
Renders items once into terminal scrollback — for build logs, CI output, streaming results
Hook for handling keyboard and mouse input events
Hook for managing component focus state within focus scopes
Hook for accessing the app instance — exit, dimensions, theme
Hook for accessing the stdout stream
Hook that reports a component's measured content rectangle (width, height)
Hook for accessing the Term instance — dimensions, capabilities, styling
Hook for reading the current theme — colors, tokens, palette name
Hook that returns current terminal dimensions (cols, rows) and re-renders on resize
Hook for virtualizing large lists — computes visible window from scroll offset and item count
Hook for accessing the runtime instance — commands, focus manager, store
Hook for showing toast notifications — returns a toast() function
Hook for readline-style line editing — history, completion, key bindings
Hook that connects a TEA state machine to React — dispatches actions, subscribes to state
Creates an in-process terminal emulator for full ANSI verification testing
Test utility — creates a headless render function with auto-cleanup
Factory for the terminal abstraction — wraps stdin/stdout, styling, and capabilities
Kernel app factory — event loop + state, the base for pipe() composition
Mount a React tree into a terminal with incremental rendering
Sugar over pipe() with sensible defaults — the simplest way to start an app
Immutable render output — text, ANSI, cell-level queries with resolved RGB colors
Compose app plugins left-to-right — createApp + withReact + withTerminal
Plugin: keyboard/mouse input mapped to named serializable commands with keybindings
Plugin: React-style event handlers (onClick, onKeyDown) with capture and bubbling
Plugin: captures process streams and terminal I/O — stdin, stdout, raw mode, signal handling
Plugin: Tab/Shift+Tab navigation, focus scopes, and focusable component registration
Plugin: base app lifecycle — dimensions, exit handling, error boundaries
A containment boundary for keyboard focus — Tab/Shift+Tab cycle within the scope
A scrollable content area that handles overflow with virtual viewport
Terminal history buffer above the visible viewport — content that scrolled off-screen
Only re-rendering cells that changed between frames — 100x+ faster than full redraws
The Elm Architecture — (action, state) → [state, effects] pattern for structured interactive state
DEC 2026 protocol for atomic screen updates — prevents visual tearing
Normal scrollback rendering — app output flows with terminal history, no alternate screen
Alternate screen rendering — preserves scrollback, absolute positioning, incremental diff
Per-node change tracking — 7 independent flags control which pipeline phases re-execute
Final rendering stage — diffs terminal buffers and emits minimal ANSI escape sequences
Content rendering stage — walks positioned tree and writes characters into a terminal buffer
Measurement + flexbox layout — computes positions, sizes, scroll offsets, and sticky elements
The core render tree node type — carries layout, content, style, and children
Terminal abstraction — wraps I/O, styling (chalk-compatible), capabilities, and dimensions
Content zone between frozen scrollback and live screen — items scroll up as new content arrives
Frozen terminal history above the dynamic zone — permanent, not re-rendered
The active render area — fullscreen apps or the bottom of inline output
The visible portion of the terminal — rows × cols currently displayed to the user
The component hierarchy — Box, Text, and custom components composed via JSX
React's engine for diffing virtual trees and applying minimal updates to the output
The process of comparing old and new React trees to determine what changed
Erase entire screen — CSI 2 J clears all visible cells without affecting scrollback
Erase screen + scrollback — CSI 3 J clears visible screen and the entire scrollback buffer
Erase entire screen — CSI 2 J clears all visible cells without affecting scrollback
Erase screen + scrollback — CSI 3 J clears visible screen and the entire scrollback buffer
Modern keyboard input protocol with key release events, modifiers, and full Unicode support
Control codes starting with ESC that tell terminals to set colors, move cursor, etc.
Pure JavaScript flexbox layout engine — powers Silvery's layout system
Headless terminal testing framework — like Playwright, but for terminal apps
Terminal feature compatibility database — caniuse.com for terminals
React-based CLI framework by Vadim Demedes — Silvery's predecessor
Lightweight state management library for React — used internally by Silvery's TEA system
React's pluggable rendering backend — Silvery uses a custom reconciler for terminal output
Facebook's WASM-based flexbox layout engine — alternative to Flexily
Latest React version — concurrent features, use() hook, server components
